summary: Backend-only testing for Shift Management API via public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. 19/19 pytest cases passed covering auth (register/login/me/bcrypt round-trip), shifts CRUD, attendance flow (status/checkin/duplicate/checkout/duration), and admin RBAC (admin 200 vs employee 403 vs unauth 401). All responses exclude MongoDB _id and password_hash.

minor.issue: allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True is inconsistent (browsers reject this combo). Not needed for mobile Bearer auth but would break web preview if cookies were used. Mobile app uses Bearer tokens so no functional impact.
No brute-force lockout/rate limiting on /api/auth/login (playbook suggests lockout after 5 fails). Not critical for mobile MVP but consider for hardening.
Return 200 instead of 201 for resource creation. Functional but not REST-idiomatic.
No validation of date (YYYY-MM-DD) or time (HH:MM) formats, nor that end_time > start_time. Raw strings accepted.

action_items: Optional: tighten CORS to explicit origins OR set allow_credentials=False since mobile uses Authorization header.
Optional: add Pydantic validation for shift date/time formats and start<end check.
Optional: add login rate-limiting / lockout for auth hardening.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is compact (~328 lines) and well-structured; no file-size concerns.
JWT_SECRET correctly read from .env; bcrypt used with gensalt (hashes will be $2b$).
All Mongo queries use {'_id': 0} projection — no ObjectId leaks.
Admin seed is idempotent: updates password if changed; creates if missing.
get_current_user correctly rejects missing/invalid/expired tokens with 401; require_admin returns 403 for non-admin.
seed_data_creation: Created transient test_emp_<uuid>@shift.com employee accounts via /api/auth/register for each test session. Admin admin@shift.com auto-seeded by backend startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL resolved from /app/frontend/.env -> EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. All 11 target endpoints verified working. Reusable pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py -v
